steps.title: Weigh the study pattern
steps.content: Many North American studies found a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury. Several studies outside North America, including Finland, found similar results, while few matched the Brazil study's lack of correlation.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content: Choice A is contradicted by studies outside North America showing the same association. Choice B invents measurement problems in Lehtinen's study. Choice D makes a claim about absolute levels in Brazil, not correlation.

steps.title: Infer the typical relationship
steps.content: Because most studies show the two levels rising and falling together and few do not, the positive association appears typical in fresh water. The answer is C.
